frame_analyses.description: Initial view shows pink Penrose drain with black suture marks and blue suture material already threaded through needle
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task appears to begin with needle already loaded and positioned

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle, approaching first mark on Penrose drain for suture placement
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate needle grasp position at 2/3 point from tip

frame_analyses.description: Needle being driven through drain tissue at first mark location
Needle passed through first mark, suture being pulled through drain tissue
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean passage through tissue, appropriate traction

frame_analyses.description: Needle repositioned for second pass through second mark on drain
Needle being driven through second mark location on drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good alignment with mark, perpendicular entry

frame_analyses.description: Beginning first throw formation - creating surgeon's knot (double throw)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right hand leading the throw formation

frame_analyses.description: Completing surgeon's knot tightening with appropriate tension
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good square knot configuration, secure tightening

frame_analyses.description: Beginning second throw with hand switch - left hand now leading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear hand switching demonstrated, alternating throw direction

frame_analyses.description: Beginning third throw with right hand leading again
Cutting suture tails with scissors inside the trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both tails being cut intracorporeally as required

penalties.description: Minor deviation from mark positioning estimated at approximately 1mm total

knot_assessments.notes: Clean surgeon's knot formation with appropriate double throw
Proper hand switching demonstrated, secure single throw
Final throw with proper alternation, knot appears secure

confidence_rationale: Clear visualization of knot sequence and hand switching. Suture placement assessment somewhat limited by camera angle, but overall technique well-observed. Completion time clearly visible.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter measurements for suture deviation due to camera angle limitations
Precise knot security assessment without stress testing
technique_summary: Excellent expert-level performance with efficient knot tying sequence and proper hand alternation. Completion time of 115 seconds places this in the expert range (80-120s). Clean technique throughout.
strengths: Rapid and secure knot tying sequence
Consistent hand switching between all throws
improvement_suggestions: Minor refinement in suture placement precision could eliminate small deviation penalties
